_description: Catalog of manual page images served from /manual-images/. Each entry maps keywords to the image file and a description of what's shown. Used by the get_manual_image tool.

images.title: Quick Start Guide — Wire Spool & Setup Steps
images.description: 6-step visual quick start guide: installing a 2lb wire spool, feeding wire through guides into the feed mechanism, plugging in the power cord, adjusting wire feed tension with the knob, replacing the nozzle and contact tip.

images.title: Quick Start Guide — Cable Setup for All Processes
images.description: Cable and polarity setup diagrams for all four processes: Stick (ground to negative, electrode to positive), MIG (ground to negative, wire feed to positive), Flux-Cored (ground to positive, wire feed to negative), TIG (ground to positive, TIG torch to negative, foot pedal inside, gas line to regulator).

images.title: How to Choose a Welder — Process Selection Chart
images.description: Full process selection chart: how to choose between Flux-Cored, MIG, Stick, and TIG based on skill level, shielding gas availability, material type, material thickness, typical applications, and weld cleanliness. Also shows MIG vs Flux-Cored comparison and duty cycle example.

images.title: Front Panel Controls Diagram
images.description: Labeled diagram of the welder's front panel showing: Home Button, Back Button, Control Knob, LCD Display, Left Knob, Right Knob, Power Switch, Storage Compartment, MIG Gun/Spool Gun Cable Socket, Positive Socket (+), Spool Gun Gas Outlet, Negative Socket (–), Wire Feed Power Cable.

images.title: Interior Controls — Wire Feed Mechanism
images.description: Interior panel showing: Cold Wire Feed Switch, Idler Arm, Feed Tensioner, Feed Roller Knob, Wire Feed Mechanism, Wire Inlet Liner, Spool Knob, Wire Feed Control Socket, Foot Pedal Socket.

images.title: Wire Spool Installation — 2lb Spool
images.description: Step-by-step diagrams for installing a 2lb wire spool: removing wingnut and spacer, placing spool on spindle against brake pad, securing with spacer and wingnut. Shows spool must unwind clockwise.

images.title: Wire Spool Installation — 10-12lb Spool + Feed Roller
images.description: Installation for 10-12lb spool using adapter, plus feed roller instructions showing V-groove for solid wire (0.025, 0.030/0.035 sizes) and knurled groove for flux-cored wire (0.045 size). How to flip/replace the feed roller.

images.title: MIG & Flux-Cored Polarity Setup (DCEP / DCEN)
images.description: Polarity diagrams for MIG (DCEP) and Flux-Cored (DCEN). MIG: Ground Clamp to Negative socket, Wire Feed Power Cable to Positive socket. Flux-Cored: Ground Clamp to Positive socket, Wire Feed Power Cable to Negative socket. Shows gas inlet and reset button location.

images.title: MIG Duty Cycle Diagram
images.description: Duty cycle diagrams for MIG welding: 120VAC = 40% at 100A (4 min weld / 6 min rest), 100% continuous at 75A. 240VAC = 25% at 200A (2.5 min weld / 7.5 min rest), 100% continuous at 115A. Explains duty cycle concept and thermal protection.

images.title: MIG/Flux-Cored Welding Technique — Gun Angles
images.description: Diagrams showing welding technique: stringer bead vs weave bead, gun angles (90° for butt welds, 45° for fillet welds), push angle (0-15°) for MIG solid wire, drag angle (0-15°) for flux-cored, and CTWD (contact tip to work distance, max 1/2 inch).

images.title: TIG Welding Setup — Cable Connections
images.description: TIG setup diagram showing cable connections: Ground Clamp Cable to Positive Socket, TIG Torch Cable to Negative Socket, Foot Pedal Cable to Foot Pedal Socket inside welder, Shielding Gas Hose to gas inlet. TIG torch and foot pedal sold separately.

images.title: TIG Torch Assembly — Tungsten Electrode Preparation
images.description: How to sharpen and assemble the TIG torch: grinding the tungsten electrode to a conical point (2.5x diameter length), electrode protrusion 1/8 to 1/4 inch beyond ceramic nozzle, collet body, collet, and back cap assembly.

images.title: Stick Welding Setup — Cable Connections
images.description: Stick welding setup: Ground Clamp Cable to Negative Socket, Electrode Holder Cable to Positive Socket. No shielding gas required. Power cord connection.

images.title: TIG and Stick Duty Cycle Diagrams
images.description: Duty cycle for TIG: 120V = 40% at 125A (4 min/6 min), 100% at 90A. 240V = 30% at 175A (3 min/7 min), 100% at 105A. Duty cycle for Stick: 120V = 40% at 80A (4 min/6 min), 100% at 60A. 240V = 25% at 175A (2.5 min/7.5 min), 100% at 100A.

images.title: Wire Weld Diagnosis — Heat Control & Penetration Overview
images.description: Overview diagrams comparing inadequate penetration (not hot enough), proper penetration (ideal heat), and excess penetration/burn-through (too hot). Shows how to increase or decrease workpiece heat. Side-by-side example weld diagrams.

images.title: Wire Weld Defects — Penetration, Adhesion, Slag
images.description: Detailed profile and top views of: proper vs inadequate penetration with causes/solutions, weld not adhering properly (gaps between bead and workpiece), coat of slag over weld explanation.

images.title: Wire Weld Defects — Porosity, Spatter, Crooked Bead, Burn-Through
images.description: Top view diagrams of: crooked/wavy bead, burn-through (hole in base material), porosity (small holes/cavities in bead), excessive spatter with causes and solutions for each.

images.title: Stick Weld Diagnosis — Heat Control Overview
images.description: Stick weld diagnosis diagrams: current too low, current too high, weld speed too fast, weld speed too slow, arc length too short, arc length too long — with corrections for each. Comparison of good stick weld to defective welds.

images.title: Stick Weld Defects — Penetration, Adhesion, Slag
images.description: Stick weld defect profile views: excess penetration/burn-through, proper penetration, inadequate penetration with causes and solutions. Weld not adhering properly. Coat of slag explanation.

images.title: Internal Wiring Schematic
images.description: Full internal wiring schematic of the Vulcan OmniPro 220 showing MCU board, Remote board, PFC, IGBT, rectifiers, transformer, solenoid valve, fans, wire feeder connections, and all CN connector labels.

images.title: Parts Assembly Diagram
images.description: Numbered exploded assembly diagram showing all 61 parts of the welder with part numbers corresponding to the parts list.
